description: Parity corpus for the chunking-block validity predicate (Codex #79 R8). Two independent gates read this same file: the server's load_build_config (tests/test_chunking_block_validity_parity.py — the REAL loader: core/builds/config.py _load_chunking) and the Console's isValidChunkingBlock (web/src/api/chunkingValidityParity.test.ts — the settings 'is this block repairable-clean or does it need a repair save' check). When they drift, a hand-written chunking block the server rejects looks clean in the settings form, its salvaged {max_chars, overlap} is never written, and every build stays blocked (the silent brick this corpus prevents — the chunking sibling of the ontology R4 / query_policy R2 bricks). base is a VALID chunking block; cases are base + set/unset mutations (dotted paths) or a whole-block replace. Only the CHUNKING key's value is under test — the parity harnesses wrap each block as {chunking: <block>}, and the absent-key (legal engine-default) state is not a case here. NOTE the one representation gap this corpus deliberately avoids: a WHOLE-number JSON float (max_chars: 500.0) parses to an int-valued JS number in the browser (no int/float distinction post-JSON.parse) so isValidChunkingBlock accepts it, while the server's _int rejects the Python float — testing it would be a false parity failure over an irreducible client-side limit. Non-whole floats (500.5) DO agree (both reject) and ARE covered. The pair relation 0 <= overlap < max_chars is NOT a config-load rule (the build defers it to the clean stage), so a bad pair is VALID here.
